title: bidens pilosa var. brachycarpa
intro: Bidens pilosa var. brachycarpa, also known as the short-fruited beggar-ticks or beggar lice, is a variety of the Bidens pilosa plant, a type of flowering plant in the aster family. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Bidens pilosa var. brachycarpa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and lower cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Bidens pilosa var. brachycarpa are typically made from the roots and leaves of the plant, which can be consumed as teas, infusions, or dried for medicinal use.
